task_completion.description: Create a Firefox bookmark folder named 'Community Health Research'
task_completion.visual_evidence: A bookmark folder named 'Community Health Research' is visible in the Firefox bookmarks toolbar, menu, or library.

task_completion.description: Save at least 4 relevant EPA pages to the bookmark folder
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Community Health Research' bookmark folder is shown containing at least 4 bookmarks to EPA pages.

task_completion.description: Download an official TRI data file (CSV, Excel, or PDF)
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Firefox downloads panel or a file manager window shows a downloaded CSV, Excel, or PDF file related to EPA TRI data.

task_completion.description: Create a summary report named tri_community_report.txt in ~/Documents/
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal shows a file named 'tri_community_report.txt' located in the Documents folder.

task_completion.description: Include Harris County data in the report with 2+ chemicals, 1+ facility, and quantitative data
task_completion.visual_evidence: The report text contains a section for Harris County, Texas, mentioning at least two specific toxic chemicals, at least one named facility, and quantitative data (like facility counts or release amounts).

task_completion.description: Include St. James Parish data in the report with 2+ chemicals, 1+ facility, and quantitative data
task_completion.visual_evidence: The report text contains a section for St. James Parish, Louisiana, mentioning at least two specific toxic chemicals, at least one named facility, and quantitative data (like facility counts or release amounts).

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to access the EPA website.
integrity.visual_evidence: Firefox or another web browser is visible navigating the EPA TRI website, rather than just using curl/wget commands.

integrity.description: The agent used actual data from the EPA website.
integrity.visual_evidence: The data in the report matches the privileged information (e.g., Harris County has 380 facilities, St. James has 11, specific chemicals match the valid list) and does not look like hallucinated placeholder text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Harris County, Texas has 380 TRI facilities; top chemicals include Ethylene, Ammonia, Toluene, and Nitrate compounds. St. James Parish, Louisiana has 11 TRI facilities; top chemicals include Ammonia, Sulfuric acid, Methanol, Nitrate compounds, and Zinc compounds. Valid chemicals include Benzene, Ethylene, Formaldehyde, Toluene, Xylene, Styrene, Butadiene, Ammonia, Hydrogen cyanide, Sulfuric acid, Hydrochloric acid, Methanol, Lead, Mercury, Dioxin, Nitrate, Zinc, Copper, Manganese.
